mission.title: Install and Configure Helm on Kubernetes
mission.description: Helm is the Kubernetes Package Manager that simplifies the process of managing Kubernetes applications through the use of Charts. Installing Helm allows you to easily deploy, manage, and share applications on your Kubernetes cluster.

steps.title: Download the Helm binary
steps.description:
Download the Helm v4.1.1 binary and add it to your PATH. Use the following commands:
```bash
wget https://get.helm.sh/helm-v4.1.1-linux-amd64.tar.gz
 tar -zxvf helm-v4.1.1-linux-amd64.tar.gz
 sudo mv linux-amd64/helm /usr/local/bin/helm
```
Make sure to replace 'linux-amd64' with the appropriate directory for your OS if you are using a different platform.

steps.title: Verify Helm installation
steps.description:
Check that Helm is installed correctly by running:
```bash
helm version
```

steps.title: Add the Helm stable repository
steps.description:
Add the official Helm stable repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add stable https://charts.helm.sh/stable
```

steps.title: Update Helm repositories
steps.description:
Update your local Helm chart repository cache:
```bash
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install a sample Helm chart
steps.description:
Install a sample application using Helm, such as Nginx:
```bash
helm install my-nginx stable/nginx --namespace default --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Nginx pod is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace default
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have Helm installed and configured, with the Nginx application deployed in your Kubernetes cluster. You can verify the deployment by checking the status of the Nginx pod.
codeSnippets:
wget https://get.helm.sh/helm-v4.1.1-linux-amd64.tar.gz

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl configured to interact with it.
